beats.text: Here's a thermostat on screen — that's your entire economy.
Three arrows point into a price level gauge: one labeled inertia, one labeled overheating, one labeled shocks. Each arrow pushes the needle up.
The overheating arrow pulses as an output gap widens — actual output climbs above a dashed potential output line.
The shock arrow spikes as an oil barrel icon slams the gauge. The needle jumps hard.
The Fed's dial appears. Turning it up raises an interest rate indicator — the output gap shrinks, the needle drops back toward a two-percent target line.
But the unemployment counter ticks upward as the economy cools. That's the cost. No free lunch.
